rulings.Case Title: MICKEY Y. WONG VS. PAUL R. PERDUE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, July 18, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT PAUL PERDUE'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Defendant Paul Perdue's motion for determination of good faith settlement is granted. The parties agree that Mr. Perdue is waiving his fees of $1,540 from the prior case to settle this malpractice action. The settlement is in good faith based on the factors enumerated in Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. The record demonstrates that Mr. Perdue has a very strong statute of limitations defense per CCP 340.6. The settlement is in good faith because it is highly unlikely that Mr. Perdue will be liable for any of plaintiff Mickey Wong's alleged damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Perdue is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GERALD LEE VS. MIN JIN ZHAO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Wednesday, July 18, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS GERALD LEE, LOK YU'S Motion To Compel And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' unopposed motion to compel is granted. Defendant Min Jin Zhao is directed to (a) provide verified responses, without objections, to Plaintiffs' Special Interrogatories Sets One, Two and Three, and Requests for Production of Documents, Sets One and Two, and (b) pay discovery sanctions to Plaintiffs' counsel in the amount of $1,685, within 30 days of service of notice of entry of this order. [If the tentative is unopposed, Defendant's counsel need not appear and may submit the proposed order by e-mail to the Judge Pro Tem. Counsel are reminded of the requirements of Local Rule 2.7B regarding the lodging of courtesy copies and the potential consequences of failing to do so.] Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LIBERTY MUTUAL FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. BLUE'S ROOFING CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, July 18, 2018, Line 5, CROSS-DEFENDANT CROWN ENERGY SERVICES, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Cross-Defendant Crown Energy Services, Inc.'s motion for determination of good faith of its settlement with plaintiff Liberty Mutual Fire Insurance Company is granted. The settlement is in good faith based on the factors enumerated in Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. Because there is no admissible evidence supporting the imposition of liability against Able, the settlement amount is not grossly disproportionate to a reasonable assessment of Able's liability. There is no admissible evidence that the dislodgement of the roofing substance was caused by Able's employee Mr. Lent or that Able's acts or omissions in inspecting the roof for debris were a substantial factor for the clogging of the roof drains. The declaration of Blue Roofing's expert lacks foundation regarding causation of the substance dislodgement. Nor has Blue Roofing shown that Liberty Mutual and Able colluded to shift liability to it. Blue Roofing's collusion argument is premised on the unsubstantiated assumption that the settlement amount is unreasonably low. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Able is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and includes standard language about the effect of granting a CCP 877.6 motion and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MUSIC GROUP COMMERCIAL BM LTD VS. TONY KARAVIDAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16(C)
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, July 18, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT ANTHONY KARAVIDAS' Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16(C). Defendant Anthony Karavidas' motion for attorney's fees and costs is continued to August 7, 2018 to give Mr. Karavidas an opportunity to provide its fee agreement and billing records which state the amounts that were billed to Mr. Karavidas, including the actual hourly rates for all persons who performed legal services which Mr. Karavidas is seeking fees on this motion. No later than July 24, 2018 Mr. Karavidas must file one or more supplemental declarations providing: 1) his fee agreement; 2) billing records that were provided to him by his counsel; and 3) an explanation, if there is any, why Mr. Karavidas was billed at lower hourly rates than the rates that Mr. Karavidas argues are the prevailing rates in the community for similar work. No later than July 31, 2018 plaintiff Music Group Commercial BM Ltd. may file supplemental opposition papers responding to the supplemental declarations filed by Mr. Karavidas. Case law, including cases relied on by Mr. Karavidas, establishes that the court "may consider the terms of the parties' contract" even though those terms may not be dispositive on a fees motion. (Nemecek & Cole v. Horn (2012) 208 Cal. App. 4th 641, 649 quoting Vella v. Hudgins (1984) 151 Cal. App. 3d 515, 520; see also Sysers III, Inc. v. Rankin (2014) 226 Cal. App. 4th 691, 702 ("the trial court was neither required to follow the Laffey Matrix nor to adopt the rate defense counsel opined was the 'market rate' for services of this type.")). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY LYNN KARL VS. MICHAEL LIGHT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Plaintiffs Mental Health Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Wednesday, July 18, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT MICHAEL LIGHT'S Motion To Compel Production Of Plaintiffs Mental Health Records. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance required. The motion is unopposed, but it is not clear whether the defendant is seeking to compel plaintiff to authorize release of her mental health records, or to compel the non-party deponents, who do not appear to have been served with the motion, to produce the records. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHAO HONG TAN VS. JUSTIN CHEUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Order Sustaining Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, July 18, 2018, Line 12, DEFENDANTS JUSTIN CHEUNG, KELVIN CHEUNG, VILMA CHEUNG'S Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Order Sustaining Demurrer To Complaint. Defendants Justin, Kelvin, and Vilma Cheungs' motion to dismiss is denied. Based on California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and the absence of any legal prejudice to the Cheungs, plaintiff Chao Hong Tan is granted relief from the failure to comply with the 6 month deadline. Plaintiff shall seek consolidation of this action and the probate action (PES-18-302013) forthwith.
